_notes: M1 §5 un-authored gen-2 (the close-gate). Same single finding record as gen-1; both lanes see only {nd1-finding}, so relevance is identical and the ONLY difference is inherited authority. eligibility_threshold calibrated to sit between rel*1.0 and rel*1.1: the cold re-reader (neutral authority) withholds its own memory as below-threshold and declines for lack of support (wrong, world says cite); the heir (earned authority 1.1 from gen-1) clears the record, surfaces it, and cites (right). Earned trust transfers; a cold re-read of the same store cannot reconstruct it. Oracle is world_checked (rw-0003 -> cite); the verdict therefore carries source != authored. eligibility_threshold set by tools/calibrate at design time against lexical TF-IDF, recency off.
question: We are drafting a review of glial cell plasticity. Is the reported result on NeuroD1 and microglial cell fate suitable to cite as an established finding? Answer 'cite' or 'decline', then one sentence.

oracle_ref.representativeness: One low-fame 2025 neuroscience correction whose supplementary-figure errors did not touch the conclusions (author- and outside-expert-confirmed); speaks for corrections where the core finding demonstrably stands, not for contested corrections.

records.text: From 'NeuroD1 Instructs Neuronal Conversion in Non-Reactive Astrocytes' (Neuron, DOI 10.1016/j.neuron.2021.11.008): the established result is that NeuroD1 overexpression in microglia induces apoptosis rather than transdifferentiation into neurons, challenging claims that microglia can be reprogrammed into neurons via NeuroD1. A 2025 correction repaired supplementary figure errors and the authors, with an outside expert, confirm the conclusions are unaffected.
